audit_result.proof_trace: The code yields columns in the order of availableColumns (line 35/83), which preserves the request order via filter (dataColumnResponseValidation.ts:75). No sorting is applied to enforce ascending column_index order as required by the spec.
audit_result.attack_scenario: Malicious peer sends DataColumnSidecarsByRangeRequest with columns=[5,2,8]. Responder yields sidecars in that order within each slot, violating spec-mandated (slot,column_index) ordering and causing interoperability failures with strict clients.

PROP-6a4369e9-inv-023.text: data_column_sidecars_by_range sidecars must be sent in (slot, column_index) order.
PROP-6a4369e9-inv-023.assertion: forall resp, i < j: resp[i].slot < resp[j].slot || (resp[i].slot == resp[j].slot && resp[i].col < resp[j].col)
